title: Fagara bombacifolia
taxonomy: Fagara bombacifolia is a species within the Rutaceae family. This family encompasses plants commonly known as citrus or rue. Fagara bombacifolia is a type of flowering plant. The Rutaceae family comprises approximately 1,500 species, spread across 150 genera. Fagara bombacifolia is native to tropical Africa.

intro: Fagara bombacifolia, also known as the Chinese prickly-ash, is a plant species native to tropical Asia, characterized by its thorny stems and aromatic leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate pain, reduce fever, and treat skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Fagara bombacifolia include limonoids, alkaloids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Fagara bombacifolia is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into herbal remedies such as cough syrups and topical creams.
